research_findings.code_auditor: 18 findings: stale design spec, experiments lifecycle undefined, phase router no fallback, launcher paused state infinite loop, research agents not adapted to codebase type, backlog priority scores never recalculated, execution subagent depends on superpowers without verification, PRD numbering fragile, meta-research prompt inline, stagnation check edge case
research_findings.product_gap: 16 findings: no reset command, no progress indicators, no skip/deprioritize backlog items, no constraint management post-onboarding, force mode no audit trail, no git repo check, no phase targeting, no rollback/undo, collaborate phase no timeout/abandon, no installation mechanism, stagnation check quality-blind
research_findings.security: 11 findings: CRITICAL shell injection in launcher.sh, uncontrolled PRD filename path traversal, system prompt exposed in process args, circular prompt injection via backlog re-injection, execution subagent unrestricted filesystem access, no state file integrity checks, TOCTOU on stop file, unbounded log accumulation, settings.local.json exposes paths, PRD numbering race condition
research_findings.market: 10 suggestions: marketplace publishing, per-cycle cost reporting, objective success metrics (Karpathy pattern), multi-agent-platform compatibility, dashboard/summary view, automated test generation, 'product-aware agent' positioning, external signal inputs, dry-run mode, OpenTelemetry trace export. Key competitor: Ralph Loop (simpler infinite loop pattern).
research_findings.customer_value: 10 findings: no installation story, no feedback loop on output quality, research phase undifferentiated across stages, target/operator codebase confusion, no progress visibility, collaboration bypassed in force mode, onboarding too long (11+ questions), PRD-to-execution loses collaboration feedback, no rollback, no multi-project identity
research_findings.experimentation: 8 findings: research agent count fixed at 7 with unknown ROI, onboarding drop-off risk, 5-lens critique unknown value, force/default binary split, priority scoring uncalibrated, single-PRD-per-cycle constraint, meta-research threshold arbitrary, stuck threshold too generous
research_findings.analytics: 12 findings: no research agent contribution tracking, no PRD critique tracking, no collaboration outcome taxonomy, no onboarding completion tracking, execution observability binary, no stagnation metrics, no backlog velocity, no error categorization, no user engagement signal, no force/default mode comparison, no aggregate dashboard, no prompt version tracking

proposed_idea: BL-007 + BL-021: Fix force mode launcher — secure shell handling and full instruction loading
validation_notes: bash -n syntax check passed, --force flag gating verified, --max-cycles rejects non-integer, paused state detection tested and prints correct message, no ${VAR} interpolation inside heredoc, heredoc delimiter single-quoted. Shell injection eliminated.
memory_updates: Added secure force mode launcher to features list
Marked BL-007 and BL-021 as completed
